title: Plain TCP Transport
text:
[REQ-0068] The agent MUST accept inbound SNMP connections over plain TCP (RFC 3430).
[REQ-0069] The agent MUST bind a TCP socket on port 10161 by default.
[REQ-0070] The agent MUST accept a configuration parameter to override the listen address and port.
[REQ-0071] The agent MUST use the BER length field to determine message boundaries on the TCP byte
           stream, as specified in RFC 3430 §3.
[REQ-0072] The agent MUST support both IPv4 and IPv6 for inbound connections.
[REQ-0073] After framing, the agent MUST silently discard any payload that does not decode as a valid
           SNMPv3 message.

Rationale: Plain TCP is a temporary transport used to enable end-to-end inbound request testing with
net-snmp client tools before TLS is introduced. It provides no authentication or encryption.
RFC-0006:C-TRANSPORT supersedes this clause.
